description: Laws, directives, and binding legal requirements — things organizations must comply with.
purpose: Reference legally binding requirements. Includes EU regulations (GDPR, AI Act, NIS2, DORA), US federal laws (HIPAA, FISMA, GLBA, CFAA), state laws (CCPA/CPRA, CalOPPA), and other national regulations.
notes: Regulations use subpath hierarchy to reference specific articles, sections, and paragraphs. Example: secid:regulation/europa.eu/gdpr#art-32/1/a for Article 32(1)(a) of GDPR. Distinct from controls — regulations are what you must comply with, controls are how you comply.
